user.title: Set up ESP32 PhotoFrame
user.description:
Enter the connection details for your PhotoFrame device.

💡 Tip: If the device appears offline and is battery-powered, press the BOOT button to wake it up.

data.host: PhotoFrame IP or hostname (e.g., 192.168.1.100 or photoframe.local)

data_description.host: The IP address or hostname of your PhotoFrame device
data_description.ha_url: The URL where Home Assistant can be accessed (used by PhotoFrame to fetch images)

error.cannot_connect:
Failed to connect to PhotoFrame. Please check the IP address/hostname and ensure the device is powered on.

💡 If battery-powered: Press the BOOT button to wake up the device.

abort.already_configured: This PhotoFrame device is already configured

init.title: Configure ESP32 PhotoFrame
init.description:
Configure Home Assistant URL and image serving options.

💡 Enable 'Use HA Images' to serve images from a camera/image entity when the device uses URL rotation mode.

data.use_ha_images: Use HA Images for URL rotation

data_description.ha_url: The URL where Home Assistant can be accessed
data_description.use_ha_images: When enabled, configures the PhotoFrame to fetch images from Home Assistant instead of an external URL
data_description.media_entity_id: Select a camera or image entity to serve to the photoframe when 'Use HA Images' is enabled
